# constantdict

An immutable dictionary class for Python, implemented as a thin layer around Python's builtin `dict` class. It is often [faster than other immutable dictionary implementations](https://matthiasdiener.github.io/constantdict/speed.html).

## Usage

Install this package with:
```
$ pip install constantdict
```

Usage example:
```python
from constantdict import constantdict

cd = constantdict({1: 2})

# constantdicts compare equal to dicts with the same items
assert cd == {1: 2}

# constantdicts are hashable, and their hashes are cached
print(hash(cd), cd)

# Attempting to modify the constantdict raises an AttributeError
try:
    # Similar for pop(), popitem(), clear(), del, and setdefault()
    cd[4] = 12
except AttributeError:
    pass
```

Please also see the [documentation](https://matthiasdiener.github.io/constantdict),
as well as the examples in the `examples/` directory.


## References

### Other packages

- [immutabledict](https://github.com/corenting/immutabledict)
- [immutables](https://github.com/MagicStack/immutables)
- [pyrsistent](https://github.com/tobgu/pyrsistent)
- [frozendict (old)](https://github.com/slezica/python-frozendict)
- [frozendict (new)](https://github.com/Marco-Sulla/python-frozendict)


### PEPs

- [PEP 416](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0416/)
- [PEP 603](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0603/)

modules.name: constantdict
documentation: Immutable dictionary implementation.

functions.name: constantdict._del_attr
documentation: Raise an AttributeError when trying to modify the object.

classes.name: constantdict.constantdict

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.fromkeys
documentation: Create a new {class}`constantdict` from supplied keys and values.

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.__hash__
documentation:
Return a hash of this {class}`constantdict`. This
{class}`constantdict` is hashable if all of its keys and values are
hashable. Once computed, the hash is cached.

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.__repr__
documentation: Return a string representation of this {class}`constantdict`.

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.__reduce__
documentation: Return pickling information for this {class}`constantdict`.

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.__or__
documentation: Return the union of this {class}`constantdict` and *other*.
Python 3.9 introduced __or__ and __ior__ for dict

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.copy
documentation: Return a shallow copy of this {class}`constantdict`.

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.set
documentation: Return a new {class}`constantdict` with the item at *key* set to *val*.
{{{ methods that return a modified copy of the dictionary

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.setdefault
documentation:
Return a new {class}`constantdict` with the item at *key* set to
*default* if *key* is not in the dictionary.

Return a reference to itself if *key* is present.

:::{note}
Based on the frozendict API.
:::

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.delete
documentation:
Return a new {class}`constantdict` without the item at *key*.

Raise a {exc}`KeyError` if *key* is not present.

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.update
documentation:
Return a new {class}`constantdict` with updated items from *other*.

:::{note}
In contrast to {meth}`dict.update`, this method does not modify the
original {class}`constantdict`, but creates a new, updated copy.
:::

```
.. doctest::

    >>> cd = constantdict(a=1, b=2)
    >>> cd_new = cd.update({"a": 10, "c": 3})
    >>> cd_new
    constantdict({'a': 10, 'b': 2, 'c': 3})
    >>> cd
    constantdict({'a': 1, 'b': 2})
```

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.discard
documentation:
Return a new {class}`constantdict` without the item at the given key.

Return a reference to itself if the key is not present.

:::{note}
Based on the {class}`pyrsistent.PMap` API.
:::

methods.name: constantdict.constantdict.mutate
documentation:
Return a mutable copy of this {class}`constantdict` as a
{class}`constantdictmutation`.

Run {meth}`constantdictmutation.finish` to convert back to an immutable
{class}`constantdict`.

:::{note}
Based on the [immutables.Map API](https://github.com/MagicStack/immutables).
:::

```
.. doctest::

    >>> cd = constantdict(a=1, b=2)
    >>> cd_mut = cd.mutate()
    >>> cd_mut["a"] = 10
    >>> del cd_mut["b"]
    >>> cd_new = cd_mut.finish()
    >>> cd_new
    constantdict({'a': 10})
    >>> cd  # unchanged
    constantdict({'a': 1, 'b': 2})
```
{{{ mutation

class_variables.name: constantdict.constantdict.__ior__
documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

class_variables.name: constantdict.constantdict.popitem
documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

class_variables.name: constantdict.constantdict.pop
documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

An immutable dictionary that does not allow modifications after
creation. This class behaves mostly like a {class}`dict`,
but with the following differences.

Additional methods compared to {class}`dict`:

```
.. automethod:: __hash__
```

```
.. automethod:: mutate
```

Methods that return a modified copy of the {class}`constantdict`:

```
.. automethod:: set
```

```
.. automethod:: setdefault
```

```
.. automethod:: delete
```

```
.. automethod:: update
```

```
.. automethod:: discard
```

Deleted methods compared to {class}`dict`
(these raise an {exc}`AttributeError` when called):

```
.. method:: __delitem__
```

```
.. method:: __setitem__
```

```
.. method:: clear
```

```
.. method:: popitem
```

```
.. method:: pop
```

classes.name: constantdict.constantdictmutation

methods.name: constantdict.constantdictmutation.finish
documentation:
Convert this object to an immutable version of itself.

```
.. doctest::

    >>> cd_mut = constantdict(a=1, b=2).mutate()
    >>> cd_mut["a"] = 12
    >>> cd = cd_mut.finish()
    >>> cd
    constantdict({'a': 12, 'b': 2})
```

A mutable dictionary that can be converted back to a
{class}`constantdict` without copying. This class behaves exactly like a
{class}`dict`, except for one additional method mentioned below.

Additional method compared to {class}`dict`:

```
.. automethod:: finish
```

classes.name: constantdict.constantdictuncachedhash

methods.name: constantdict.constantdictuncachedhash.mutate
documentation:
Return a mutable copy of this {class}`constantdict` as a
{class}`constantdictuncachedhashmutation`.

Run {meth}`constantdictuncachedhashmutation.finish` to convert back to an
immutable {class}`constantdict`.

A {class}`constantdict` that does not cache its hash
value. This is useful when the dictionary contains items that are not
immutable and whose hash value might therefore change.

```
.. automethod:: mutate
```

classes.name: constantdict.constantdictuncachedhashmutation

methods.name: constantdict.constantdictuncachedhashmutation.finish
documentation: Convert this object to an immutable version of itself.

A mutable dictionary that can be converted back to a
{class}`constantdictuncachedhash` without copying. This class behaves
exactly like a {class}`dict`, except for one additional method mentioned
below.

Additional method compared to {class}`dict`:

```
.. automethod:: finish
```
